transcript_text:
spk_0: Alrighty... Okay, so Lee, can you tell me a bit about yourself?
spk_0: What are you doing at the moment?
spk_0: Are you studying? Are you graduated?
spk_0: What's... what's your life look like?
spk_1: Yeah, so I'm a second-year animation student at IDT.
spk_1: So, it's a full-time course.
spk_1: So, I'm in college four days out of five, and it's really intensive...
spk_1: so I come in at nine in the morning and then I go home at like five, five or six.
spk_1: And yeah, it's a very project-heavy course.
spk_1: So, there's a lot of overtime, a lot of staying at college past the, like, class hours.
spk_0: Is that what a typical week looks like for you?
spk_1: Yeah, so I'd be in college from Monday to Thursday, and then on Friday, we have online classes.
spk_1: And then, yeah, over the weekend, it's just project work and project work...
spk_1: getting stuff done.
spk_1: Our semesters are only like 12 weeks long, so it's very quick...
spk_1: flies by.
spk_0: Okay, fantastic. And so, what are your contact hours versus independence that they...
spk_1: Sorry... could you rephrase the question?
spk_0: Yeah, it's just in regards to, like, how much time do you spend in contact with, like, lecturers or, like, in class versus, you know, doing independent work.
spk_1: So, in class, I'd say it would be like...
spk_1: I mean, we have six hours of class every day, so that would be about, like, 20 to 30 hours in total across the week.
spk_1: And then, I mean, lectures sometimes don't come in, but most of that time would be spent directly with lectures, and then maybe three hours a day over time each day in college getting the work done.
spk_0: Well, okay... so it sounds like you're very busy.
spk_0: So, what's your timetable structure look like?
spk_1: Yeah, no, it's just a class in the morning, which is three hours, and then a class in the evening, which is also three hours.
spk_1: Those would be mandatory.
spk_1: So it wouldn't be studio work; it would be like the lecture teaching.
spk_1: Yeah, very little free time.
spk_1: So, like, anyone who has a job in our course has to work on, like, a Friday or on the weekend.
spk_1: Otherwise, it just doesn't work with our timetable.
spk_0: Yikes! Okay, what about any extracurricular activities?
spk_0: Sports, societies...
spk_0: things like that?
spk_1: Yeah, yeah, so that would all be after college.
spk_1: So, like, I'm... I'm in, like, two sports societies.
spk_1: I do Taekwondo, so that would be three times a week, which I've not been going to as much with all the work.
spk_1: But it's like it would be Monday, Thursdays, and Fridays, and then badminton is on Fridays and the weekend as well because, yeah, most people are just busy during the week.
spk_0: Wow! Okay, fair play...
spk_0: Yeah. And then, who do you live with at the moment?
spk_1: I live with my parents...
spk_1: I live in Dublin. It's very accessible.
spk_0: Yeah, brilliant! That's great.
spk_0: So, who usually makes decisions around food in your household?
spk_1: It would be my... my parents.
spk_1: My mom does a lot of the cooking, but she's very busy.
spk_1: Like, both my parents are working full-time as well, so, like, a lot of the time we would have to order food home.
spk_0: Okay, perfect! So, would you say that they deal with most of the cooking in the house?
spk_1: Yeah, definitely...
spk_1: yeah.
spk_0: Perfect. So, would you get involved with any of the groceries at all, or is that mainly all them?
spk_1: I think my parents would do the groceries, but, like, I have a younger sibling, so...
spk_1: So, like, sometimes if they're not home in the evenings, I'll be doing the cooking as well.
spk_0: Okay, great! Okay, I want to chat a bit about some social media platforms, then...
spk_0: What platforms do you typically use?
spk_1: I'm on Instagram and TikTok, I guess, the most.
spk_1: I think those are the most frequent...
spk_1: yeah. For social media, I wouldn't really be on anything else.
spk_0: Okay, perfect. And if you were then able to order them from most used to least used...
spk_0: how would you order them?
spk_1: I feel like, for contacts, it would be Instagram, but for, like, time I spend in the day, it would be TikTok.
spk_0: Okay, interesting...
spk_0: Do you know roughly how much time you do spend on social media?
spk_1: I say one to two hours in a day...
spk_1: Like, yeah, I'd say, like, across everything, I probably spend two hours—TikTok, YouTube kind of thing.
spk_1: I'm really bad at responding on social media, so I don't spend, like, a lot of time texting.
spk_1: But I guess other platforms, like Discord, just for college work...
spk_1: I would be contacting people in my class on that a lot as well.
spk_1: But yeah, I'd only say two to three hours; if it's a weekend, maybe five.
spk_0: Okay, perfect.
spk_1: Thank you.
spk_0: Okay, so, where would you say you tend to see the most food-related content online?
spk_1: Definitely TikTok.
spk_0: Okay... and do you follow any food influencers or creators?
spk_1: I know there's a lot of creators who do, like, the meal planning or, like, easy-to-make meals across the week...
spk_1: I definitely follow a lot of those kind of 'how to make it' or, like, the recipe videos.
spk_0: Okay. And what about the likes, like Food Review Club, and like, you know, going around testing takeaways and restaurants and things like that?
spk_1: Probably, yeah... like, I'd see a lot of those videos as well.
spk_1: Especially, like, influencers going around Dublin or, like, the UK to different, like, street food places.
spk_0: Okay... and have you ever ordered food because you saw it on social media?
spk_1: I think so, yeah. I saw Jeanne's Kitchen on social media, and then, like, the next week, my friends and I went to it.
spk_1: I think a lot of people will hear about a place, and if it's nearby, we definitely go to it if we've seen it or heard about it somewhere nice.
spk_0: Was that for the spice bags?
spk_1: Yeah, yeah.
spk_0: Has to be done. Okay, so what was it being promoted then?
spk_0: Was it a deal or offer that you were interested in, or was it just like...
spk_0: going to try that spice bug?
spk_1: I think I'm going to try, but I think a lot of the time there will also be student deals, like, especially with, like, pizza and takeaway kind of places.
spk_1: If there's a student deal or if there's, like, a big offer in our household, we'll be much more inclined to then be like, since there's a deal, to get it that day, rather than, I don't know, the next day.
spk_0: Okay, so... walk me through a typical weekday, from morning to night.
spk_0: What do you eat, when, and where?
spk_1: I think on a weekday I'll either have breakfast at home, which would be like, I don't know, a sandwich or something, or if I eat in college, I won't be eating lunch that day.
spk_1: I think college... the...
spk_1: the food is much cheaper than, like, going to a supermarket, but it's still very expensive.
spk_1: So I'll either have...
spk_1: if I have breakfast at home, then I'll have lunch at college just because it's a lot easier than packing a lunch or then, like, I don't know, eating cold lunch, especially if it's a really long day or if, like, I'm going to something afterwards.
spk_1: I think a lot of the time after college I'll also go to, like, I don't know, a convenience store or somewhere and, like, get the pot noodles or, like, a sandwich as well.
spk_1: Just because, like, if there's something that's really easy and quick to eat, I'd be much more inclined to do that.
spk_1: Especially if it's, like, a really long day and then, yeah, I'd probably get home around 6 or, like, it depends.
spk_1: If I have something on that day, I'll be home around nine, and at that point, it's like, dinner's cold...
spk_1: it's just... yeah, okay, perfect!
spk_0: What about weekends, then?
spk_0: How does that differ from weekdays to weekends?
spk_1: On weekends, my parents will make breakfast, so it's like proper nutrition and everything.
spk_1: It's great! And then I'd usually spend most of the weekend at home.
spk_1: And then, like, if it's...
spk_1: I don't know if it's a Friday or a Saturday, we might order takeaway as well, just so that it's not like...
spk_1: I don't know, just to make it easier to, like, celebrate the end of the week.
spk_0: Absolutely! What would you say you look for, then, when you're deciding what to eat?
spk_1: I guess... what's quick and easy?
spk_1: Like... like, it's easier to order from the takeaway nearby than, like, I don't know, ordering from the city center and stuff?
spk_1: I guess. Yeah, we kind of find, like, we have, like, a rotation, so we just kind of switch between what we haven't had recently.
spk_0: Okay. And is that just, like, in the house?
spk_0: Like, you have Chinese one week, you have Indian the next...
spk_0: sort of thing?
spk_1: Yeah, yeah... yeah.
spk_0: Okay, great! So, how often, then, would you say that you cook?
spk_1: Probably, like, personally, probably only once...
spk_1: once a week. Like, especially now, like, during college.
spk_1: It gets really busy when college ends.
spk_1: Maybe I'll do more of the household cooking because my parents will be busy.
spk_1: But a lot of the time during the week, we'll get, like, the ready meals, like lasagna or, like, cottage pie from Tesco and just eat that for dinner as well.
spk_0: Great... okay. With that being said, what does cooking mean to you?
spk_1: Usually, it means, like, something that's, like, homemade.
spk_1: Like, I didn't just take something from the fridge and, like, microwave it or put it in the oven.
spk_1: So when I cook, it'll be, like, pasta or something and just kind of making sure it's balanced, that there's, like, something filling and then, like, a protein and, like, salad or something.
spk_1: So I feel like cooking feels like a big task because usually it takes a while to do, but, like, if we're just doing, like, a ready meal, I wouldn't call that cooking.
spk_0: Okay, and then... how often would you order food or groceries for delivery?
spk_1: I think ordering food would be, like, once a week, and then groceries...
spk_1: It's only like... we haven't ordered groceries since quarantine.
spk_1: Like, it's... has...
spk_1: hasn't been much need for that in our household.
spk_0: Okay, great. So, what meals would you say are the most stressful, or feel the most stressful, to make?
spk_1: Yeah, I don't know.
spk_1: I think breakfast is always, like, stressful because, like, I don't know, you're...
spk_1: you're always busy or, like, worried about whether or not you have everything for class or for work.
spk_1: I'd say that that's something that definitely gets skipped.
spk_1: Or, like, definitely is not as, like, what...
spk_1: what it should be. But I think dinners can also be a bit stressful, especially, like, if it's been a couple days of eating the same thing—like three days of soup—you kind of get fed up with eating the same thing.
spk_0: Absolutely! Well, then, which meals feel most enjoyable?
spk_1: Definitely... like homemade meals, especially when my parents cook.
spk_1: Like, they do it a lot better than I do.
spk_1: I'd say homemade meals are definitely great.
spk_1: Yeah!
spk_0: And then, when does food feel functional, social, or comforting?
spk_1: I guess, like, getting takeaway with friends or, like, I feel like when you're going out with friends, it's not as...
spk_1: like, it doesn't feel as bad getting a pizza or getting a spice bag.
spk_1: And, like, it definitely feels more like a social thing, being able to...
spk_1: like, rather than, like, ordering it to your house and, like, eating it on your own.
spk_1: Being able to go somewhere and sit at, like, a table with people and, like...
spk_1: I don't know, that feels a lot more sociable.
spk_1: It feels much more like...
spk_1: not as guilty.
spk_0: What about... functional and comforting?
spk_1: Yeah, I think... I think it does depend, like, how often we're ordering because, like, if we've ordered takeaway twice in a week, then it, like...
spk_1: the second time, it doesn't feel as comfortable...
spk_1: comforting. It feels a bit like I should have made something myself.
spk_0: Absolutely. Okay...
spk_0: Yeah. So, what about groceries then?
spk_0: Who does the groceries in the house?
spk_0: And is it normally in person then?
spk_1: Yeah, my parents would be doing the groceries on it...
spk_1: would be in person.
spk_0: Okay... do you know roughly how many times they go grocery shopping in a week?
spk_1: I'd say, like, twice a week.
spk_1: I think we forget a lot of things.
spk_1: And, like, there's also a lot of grocery shops which wouldn't have the same things.
spk_1: So we have, like, a lot of Polish products in our house, which, like, Tesco wouldn't sell.
spk_1: So we'd have to make a couple of runs to different stores.
spk_0: Okay... makes sense.
spk_0: Do you know roughly how much you'd spend, then, on food in a typical week?
spk_1: In a week? I think...
spk_1: I know. We're in a house of four.
spk_1: I think groceries go like €100 per trip.
spk_1: Like, it's expensive, Anya!
spk_1: Usually, it's like things like eggs and milk.
spk_1: I'd say in a week, like €200, depending on the amount we go to the shop.
spk_0: Absolutely. What about you personally, then, Lee?
spk_0: How much would you be spending in...?
spk_1: A typical week between, like, college lunches?
spk_1: Probably, like, €30.
spk_1: I eat too much at college, so, like, I'd be spending, like, five euro a day, which would then, like...
spk_1: anything after college would also kind of amount.
spk_1: So that would be, like, 30, €40.
spk_1: And that's, like, on a quiet week.
spk_1: If I'm, like, spending time with friends after college, that would definitely get a lot more expensive, like €80, depending on the week.
spk_0: Okay, and do you have, like, a set budget for the week, or does it change?
spk_1: No, no, I work very much, kind of on and off, especially in the summer.
spk_1: I do, like, festivals and concerts.
spk_1: So, like, I'd work a bit in summer, and then that would kind of be...
spk_1: Yeah, whatever. Jobs come and go, I guess.
spk_1: So, like, my budgeting is very unstable for the year.
spk_0: Absolutely. Okay, so your budget then—your weekly budget—if you were to split that between food deliveries, your groceries, and eating out, what would you say the split is?
spk_1: Sorry... between food deliveries and groceries?
spk_0: Yeah... so, takeaways, groceries, and eating out.
spk_1: I said takeaway would be around, like, 40 to 60 Euro, depending on where we're ordering from.
spk_1: And then, gross groceries...
spk_1: yeah, household groceries would be like 100 to €200.
spk_1: And then, like, I go to the store sometimes, like, if...
spk_1: if it's a weekend and my mom's forgotten the eggs or something, I'd also go to the store.
spk_1: So, like, my personal groceries would probably be like €20 a week.
spk_0: Okay, great. So, what reasons do you normally order food deliveries...
spk_0: or takeaways?
spk_1: Like, on a Friday, I guess when it's the end of the week and everyone's really tired, or, like, on a day when we don't have many groceries in the fridge, it's just easier to order food than to, like, go to the grocery store at 9 p.m.
spk_1: Absolutely.
spk_0: Is Friday the most likely day you'll get takeout?
spk_1: I'd say so, yeah...
spk_0: Is that, like, a ritual in the house?
spk_1: Oh, it's like an unspoken ritual...
spk_1: We're not meant to call it a 'ritual,' but it definitely is.
spk_0: Okay. And does getting a takeaway feel like a treat?
spk_0: Does it feel like a habit, or does it feel like just, like, a normal part of your spending?
spk_1: I'd say it feels special, but if it gets too frequent...
spk_1: yeah, it feels like a habit.

spk_0: you... for you to paint me a picture of what was happening that day.

spk_1: Well, I haven't ordered for them in a while.
spk_1: We usually order directly from, like, the places we go to, but we wanted to try something new.
spk_1: And that was, I guess, the main reason we ordered from Just Eat, because there's so many options.
spk_1: It was like... I don't know...
spk_1: it wasn't just Chinese or Indian.
spk_1: It was like everything.
spk_1: So we ordered from there, and we haven't ordered from there because our neighbor...
spk_1: actually, I live in an apartment, and our neighbor took our takeaway that day.
spk_1: And yeah, we were...
spk_1: we were told like, oh, well, that was about it.
spk_1: So... yeah, but it was...
spk_1: it was genuinely just because we wanted to try something new that day, and it seemed like the easiest place to go to.
spk_1: And I also feel like there was, like, this ad where they sing a song for Just Eat going around at that time.
spk_1: And, like, all my friends would sing it, like, every five minutes.
spk_1: And it was definitely just, like, the first thing that popped into my head.
spk_0: Interesting... Okay, so when the order was delivered, your neighbor took it in?
spk_1: Yeah, just like someone just walked up and took it.
spk_1: And then we were told that, like, we...
spk_1: we took the food, and we're like, 'Like, clearly not.
spk_1: We didn't.' And we had to order from, like, our usual Chinese that day...
spk_1: disaster!
spk_0: Okay, and so the other takeaways you order from...
spk_0: you, you do that directly via phone, is it?
spk_1: Yeah.

spk_0: was it like a planned order, or was it spontaneous?
spk_1: It was... spontaneous.
spk_0: What would you say, then, triggers you to order a takeaway?
spk_1: Yeah, just kind of like...
spk_1: yeah, when? Like just when you want to have something different...
spk_1: like not the same home meal, I guess, or just...
spk_1: yeah. Just a really long day when you're really tired.
spk_1: It just, like, doesn't feel like cooking.
spk_1: We'd order takeaway.
spk_0: Okay, are there go-to orders for you, or do you like variety?
spk_1: Like, we'd have the same order for each place, and we'd have it, like, saved...
spk_1: I think on my dad's phone.
spk_1: So, like, we know exactly what we'd get.
spk_0: Okay, interesting. So, for example, you'd go directly to the Chinese, but he has, like, the order written out on, like, a note on his phone.
spk_1: Yeah.

spk_0: Would you say your ordering habits have changed over time?

spk_1: over years. Like, I wouldn't say it's like...
spk_1: something very new.
spk_0: Okay... in, like, the last six months, has anything changed for you?
spk_1: I started ordering duck from the Chinese place...
spk_1: like, that's been a change.
spk_1: I think we got a bit tired of ordering a spice bag, but...
spk_0: Yeah, right. And you mentioned you don't order groceries for delivery, so you, in particular...
spk_0: is there a reason why you don't order groceries for delivery?
spk_1: Me specifically? We have, like, a convenience store, like, five minutes from where we live.
spk_1: Like, it's just easier.
spk_1: And, like, even, like, a lot of the time, my dad will tell me to pick up groceries on the way from college.
spk_1: And, like, it's like a 10-15 mile walk from, from college to a grocery store...
spk_1: Like, it feels easier to just go there and grab what you need rather than calling for delivery.
spk_0: Can you think of a time when grocery delivery might've made your life easier?
spk_1: Yeah, my dad was living in Switzerland a couple of years ago, and my mom doesn't drive, and the shop next to us didn't exist back then, so we had to order delivery like every week.
spk_0: Well, okay... fair play.
spk_0: And did you find it useful at the time?
spk_1: Definitely, yeah, because it would have been like...
spk_1: would have been like a 40-minute walk to the nearest grocery store.
spk_1: It was definitely saving time.
spk_0: Okay, what would you say would be the...
spk_0: the perfect grocery delivery app for students?
spk_1: Grocery delivery app?
spk_1: I don't know... I feel like a lot of the stores have their own apps.
spk_1: I'd say something that's really accessible and easy to use, and I'd probably say, like...
spk_1: I don't know... like, I know Lidl does, like, these discounts, and so does Tesco.
spk_1: I think students always kind of go more towards what's cheaper, in the nicest way possible.
spk_1: Like, I think really, really expensive supermarkets we usually stay away from.
spk_0: Okay, is there any time where grocery shopping feels stressful or overwhelming to you?
spk_1: Yeah, I think, like, when too many things pile into the grocery basket, and it kind of starts becoming like...
spk_1: like when you start counting out how much it costs and realizing this: it was meant to be a €10 trip, and now it's like a lot more than that.
spk_1: I think that gets stressful.
spk_0: What about... what about promotions and discounts?
spk_0: Would they make you consider grocery delivery more frequently?
spk_1: Probably, yeah. Like, if I'm in a grocery store and there's, like, a discount on something, I'll be more inclined to buy that.
spk_1: And kind of like, in my head, I'm like, I've just saved, I don't know, five euro.
spk_1: And then, like, maybe I didn't, but I'd definitely be more tempted to get it.
spk_0: Interesting... So, does that theory still apply, then, for grocery deliveries?
spk_1: I'd say, if it, like...
spk_1: if it was significantly cheaper...
spk_1: Yeah! I think it also just depends where people are located, if they're near a store, because, like, if it's quicker to deliver, it definitely makes sense to do that.
spk_0: Interesting... Thank you!
spk_0: So, which food delivery apps do you currently use?
spk_1: Like Domino's or Apache?
spk_1: I'd say, like, apps that are directly linked to the takeaway company rather than apps that are, like, across the board.
spk_1: I think it's just because we live in an apartment...
spk_1: if there's ever an issue with a delivery or, like, I don't know, if the delivery driver doesn't arrive, it's much harder for us to dispute that through Just Eat.
spk_1: While with a direct takeaway place, they can be like, 'I know my driver and all that,' and it's much easier to figure out what happened.
spk_0: Absolutely! And... have you ever used Delivery or Uber Eats?

spk_1: like... Yeah, I'd say so.
spk_1: And, like, I think they're just really recognizable.
spk_1: While, like, Uber Eats, I'd associate more with, like, Uber, the taxis.
spk_0: What about... what about delivery?
spk_0: What... what stands out to you about them?
spk_1: I don't know... I just...
spk_1: I always see them, like, cycling places, but I didn't actually know they did food deliveries for, like, ages.
spk_1: I thought it was just, like...
spk_1: I don't know, another postal service.
spk_1: I'll be honest. Okay...
spk_1: I don't see many ads for delivery.
spk_0: Interesting... So, you thought it was like a courier service?
spk_1: Yeah, like, because I feel like the bugs are blue...
spk_1: It doesn't really say anything about what they do.
spk_1: And like, just eat...
spk_1: It's in the name. Uber Eats as well.
spk_1: And like Deliveroo...
spk_1: I was like, well, the delivering something could be like packages, you know, Amazon or something.
spk_0: Okay, that's super interesting.

spk_0: So, the first one is price; second is offers and promotions; third is speed; fourth is habit; and subscription to benefits is five.

spk_1: I think the deals would probably be number one, and then, I guess, price would be number two.

spk_1: Then I'd probably say, 'Habit...

spk_1: I'd say, like... I'd say it's pretty good because there's so much competition on Just Eat.
spk_1: I'd probably say five.

spk_1: I haven't seen any on Just Eat.
